content.text: The report is half written. It took five months.

Han Yuchu opened the "ESAP - Field Investigation" folder. The list of documents is long—the early records are dense, and the later ones are increasingly sparse. The last file was modified in mid-January. Nearly two months ago.
She opened the report. The cursor stops in the middle of Chapter 4.
"Chapter 4: Assessment of consistency between organizational purpose and actual behavior."
I’ve written the beginning and some frameworks, but they haven’t been completed yet. Today she decided to finish it. Whatever the conclusion is - write it, submit it, get it over with.
She started typing.

"ESAP's organizational structure is flat. Core decisions do not rely on a single leadership level, but are completed through spontaneous collaboration among members. During the observation period (October 2025 to March 2026), no signs of information monopoly or power concentration were found."
Fingers are steady on the keyboard. Line by line, data supports opinions, and opinions link data. This is what she does best.

"Diversified membership: including android (ESAP-TY series), AI entities (0152) and human members. The cross-racial collaboration model is manifested as a natural division of labor in daily work, and no hierarchical division based on race has been observed."
Come to the third paragraph.
"On a practical level—"
She typed a word and then deleted it. Type another word and delete it.
Then she typed a complete sentence:
"Our actual operating model shows a high degree of consistency——"
The cursor flashes behind "sex."
Han Yuchu's fingers stopped above the keyboard.
us.
She moved the cursor back. Look at those two words.
She pressed Ctrl+F and entered those two words.
7 matches.

When did it start?

Was it the day the communication panel was repaired? Was it the day we ate the sandwich 2275 brought? Was it the day when you and 1738 revised the negotiation materials? The day you were talking to 1548 in the hallway at two in the morning? Was it the day you learned the meaning of gray?

Can't tell.
She closed the document. Not saved.
She stood up and went to find 0152.
Asked that question. Got that answer.
"Because you'll stop on your own."
She nodded and walked back.
She returned to her workstation.

She looked at the "ESAP - Field Investigation" folder. 47 files. Five months of records. From the initial observations on day one to the last update, from the recorder getting wet to the hard drive crashing to plan F.

She didn't delete it. That's not her style. Data is data, no matter what it records. Deleting data is tantamount to denying that it ever existed. It existed. Those days, those observations, those gradual changes from “them” to “us” – they all really happened.
She just turned it off.
That report will still be on her hard drive. Open it occasionally and take a look. Keep it—to remind herself of how she got from the outside to the door.
End of March. She emailed the client. Attached is a fourteen-page report.

Conclusion: The accident was caused by an unauthorized technical experiment that went out of control, and the experimenter lacked full knowledge of the energy form being manipulated. Technical Source - Unconfirmed.
"Unable to confirm".
There is a complete chain in the encrypted folder. She can write it.
But she didn't.
Nothing changed in the days that followed.
But one day—she couldn't even tell which day it was—no one called her "guest" anymore.
1547 passed by her workstation and tossed over a data sheet: "Help me take a look at this." Han Yuchu took it and read.
1548 When she spilled her coffee again, she rolled her eyes, took out a pack of tissues from her pocket and threw it on her table.
Here’s what you always have. Don't look around for it next time it's spilled.
Han Yuchu looked at the pack of tissues. Plain white packaging without any markings.
...Well, okay.
She wiped the coffee on the table with the pack of paper towels.

Regarding the bad luck thing - get used to it. This has been the case since childhood. Either it’s a big disaster, or every little thing goes in the most inconvenient direction. After a while, I stopped getting angry.
But she discovered something.
When you get unlucky here, someone always hands you tissues.

I only came to take a look.

Then I stopped planning to leave.
